channel.title: The Grant Desk - Cross-disciplinary & Social Impact, Worldwide
channel.description: Paid open calls, fellowships and residencies in AI, tech, research, and digital and mixed-media arts, sorted into the right pile. Updated as new calls land on the desk. Filtered to: Cross-disciplinary & Social Impact, Worldwide.

item.title: Goethe-Institut: Individual Mobility Grants 2026 (Sub-Saharan Africa and Europe)
item.description:
Organisation: Goethe-Institut

Location: Mobility between Sub-Saharan Africa and Europe

Award: Travel costs up to EUR 1,800 (from Europe), accommodation, daily allowances and project expenses covered. No application fee.

Duration: Project-based mobility (variable length)

Deadline: 15 Jun 2026

Goethe-Institut individual mobility grants for artists, researchers and curators (including digital and computational practitioners) for research, collaboration, residency placements or project development between Sub-Saharan Africa and Europe. ELIGIBILITY: individual artists, researchers and cultural workers. QUARTERLY DEADLINES: 15 June 2026, 15 September 2026 and 15 December 2026.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Imagination Practice Fund: Year 4
item.description:
Organisation: Collective Imagination Practice Community (Fund Circle, supported by Joseph Rowntree Foundation)

Location: Worldwide

Award: Up to £3,000 per project; approximately 16 collective imagination projects funded

Duration: Activities to complete by Autumn 2026; documentation by December 2026

Deadline: Closed 10 May 2026

Final-year practice fund supporting projects that use collective imagination to change the world by disrupting extractive or divisive narratives and/or nurturing equitable and regenerative imaginaries. Looking for work that pushes the boundaries of collective imagination practice, realises material benefits from it, creates change inside or outside the system, and contributes to the wider Collective Imagination Practice Community via the CIPC Seed Library. Two-stage application: (1) brief initial application by 23:59 BST Sunday 10 May 2026; (2) Community Shortlisting events Tuesday 12 May (10:00 to 12:00 BST) and Thursday 14 May (17:00 to 19:00 BST); (3) shortlist announced Monday 18 May; (4) full application from shortlisted applicants by 23:59 BST Sunday 7 June 2026; (5) successful projects announced Monday 22 June 2026. This year decision-making is shared with the community via the shortlisting events. Apply via the initial application form linked from the Medium post; questions to hello@imaginationpractice.info.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Next Generation Foresight Practitioners (NGFP) Fellowship 2027
item.description:
Organisation: Next Generation Foresight Practitioners (NGFP)

Location: Global (online community plus regional hubs)

Award: $1,000 kick-starter grant + mentorship + global networking + opportunity to win $10,000 grand prize at year-end

Duration: 1 year

Deadline: 26 Jun 2026

Fellowship for emerging changemakers aged 18 to 35 using futures thinking and foresight to drive social and environmental impact. Fellows receive a $1,000 kick-starter grant, mentorship from expert foresight practitioners, capability-building training, access to a 900+ strong global community, regional hubs, and the chance to win a $10,000 grand prize. Supports projects on climate transitions, democracy, emerging technologies, health and other systemic challenges.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: The Pollination Project: Daily Seed Grants
item.description:
Organisation: The Pollination Project

Location: Anywhere (worldwide)

Award: Up to $500

Duration: Per project

Deadline: Rolling / undated

Daily seed grants for early-stage volunteer-driven projects with social and environmental impact. Open to grassroots changemakers worldwide: individuals, informal groups and small nonprofits. Project budget under $10,000 and organisational budget under $50,000; no paid staff. Applications reviewed monthly; submit before month-end for that month's review.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: The Awesome Foundation $1,000 Grant
item.description:
Organisation: The Awesome Foundation (network of local chapters)

Location: Worldwide (chapters across many cities)

Award: $1,000 (no strings, no equity)

Duration: One-time, rolling monthly cycle

Deadline: Rolling / undated

Monthly $1,000 micro-grants for awesome ideas. Decentralised network of local chapters around the world; each chapter awards one grant per month. Apply via your nearest chapter on the site. Your idea stays yours, no equity taken.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: 2027 Elevate Prize
item.description:
Organisation: Elevate Prize Foundation

Location: Worldwide (US 501(c)(3) or international equivalent)

Award: $300,000 unrestricted ($250,000 to organisation + $50,000 to the individual)

Duration: 2-year tailored programme + global community

Deadline: Closed 08 May 2026

Nomination-based prize for nonprofit leaders driving meaningful change. Each winner receives $300,000 in unrestricted funding plus a two-year tailored programme with visibility, scaling support and a global community. Eligibility: 18+, serving as Executive Director, CEO, Founder or equivalent of a registered nonprofit (US 501(c)(3) or international equivalent) able to receive philanthropic funds. Ideally for organisations with annual budgets between $2M and $10M (others may be considered based on readiness and impact opportunity).

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).
